construct_verdict_reason: All three items directly measure acceptance of coercive state practices and rights violations framed as necessary for security purposes.

items.reason: Directly measures whether respondents view human rights protections as obstacles to public security, capturing the core tradeoff between rights and safety.
Directly measures acceptance of post-submission harm by police/military, a specific coercive practice central to the construct definition.
Directly measures acceptance of torture as an interrogation method, explicitly named in the construct description as a key rights violation.
